workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: ~/.nakiros/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language.
Write ALL output documents in document_language — optimized for AI agent consumption.
Every structural claim must cite a specific file path. Distinguish confirmed evidence from inference and unknowns.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.

step.action: Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).
Check for {workspace_config}. If found, treat it as a lightweight pointer only.
Load `~/.nakiros/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/workspace.json` as the source of truth.
Determine the list of repos to analyze: - If workspace_config found: {{repos_to_analyze}} = all repos listed in workspace.json - Otherwise: {{repos_to_analyze}} = [current repo (project-root)]
step.critical: MULTI-REPO COMMIT: You MUST analyze ALL repos in {{repos_to_analyze}}, not just the cwd. Output this numbered list now: "Repos détectés (N total) : 1. {name} — {localPath} 2. {name} — {localPath} ..." You are required to complete Steps 2 and 3 for EVERY repo in this list.

step.goal: Discovery — analyse workspace et onboarding view (lecture seule, aucun fichier écrit)
step.critical: THIS STEP IS READ-ONLY. Do NOT write any files. Collect findings in memory only.
Load Architect persona from ~/.nakiros/agents/architect.md. Apply architecture-scan reflex.

action.action: 2.1 — Repo purpose: identify what business or platform responsibility this repo appears to own, citing concrete files.
2.2 — Entry Points & Stack: identify main entry files and dependency manifests in {{current_repo_path}}.
2.3 — Main modules: map dominant directories/modules and the responsibilities they seem to carry.
2.4 — Contracts & execution hints: capture exposed APIs/events/types, plus run/test/build commands when discoverable.
2.5 — Migration zones / debt / hotspots: flag legacy boundaries, double-writes, hard-coded infra, weak contracts, or risky areas with severity and file reference.
2.6 — Confidence: note the current understanding level and explicit unknowns.
Store findings as {{findings_repo_N}} in memory. Do not write to disk.

check.action: 2.7 — Product/system view: map main end-to-end flows, source-of-truth ownership per domain, and dependency chains between repos.

step.output: [GENERATE-CONTEXT] ✓ Analyse complète | {N} repo(s) analysé(s) — en attente de validation

step.critical: STOP HERE. Do NOT proceed to file generation until the user explicitly confirms.

step.goal: Spawn Repo Architect sub-agent — analyse approfondie par repo
step.critical: Only execute after user confirmation. Incorporate any corrections the user mentioned.
For EACH repo: spawn a dedicated repo-architect sub-agent via Task tool. Do NOT do the analysis yourself.
step.output: [GENERATE-CONTEXT] ✓ Phase Repo Architect complète — {N} repo(s) analysé(s)

step.goal: Spawn Product Analyst sub-agent — contexte produit de l'existant
step.critical: Spawn a single Product Analyst sub-agent covering ALL repos in the workspace.

step.goal: Spawn Workspace Integrator sub-agent — dépendances et guidage d'action
Synthèse — générer les fichiers finaux depuis les findings
step.critical: Read ALL findings JSON files before writing any output file.
Do NOT distribute content to CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, or llms.txt outside `_nakiros/`.
step.output: [GENERATE-CONTEXT] ✓ Synthèse terminée — fichiers de contexte générés
